user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show the claimed object (car) and the claimed part (rear bumper area) with visible damage.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims a dent on the back of the car, specifically the rear bumper area. Image img_1 clearly shows significant denting and deformation on the rear bumper and trunk area of the silver car, consistent with the claim.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows damage on the front bumper of a car, which aligns with the user's claim.
claim_status_justification: The user claims a scratch on the front bumper. Image img_1 clearly shows a scratch and scuff marks on the front bumper of a white car. Image img_2 shows a different car model (Jaguar) and no visible damage, but img_1 directly supports the claim.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows the claimed damage on the windshield.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows a shattered windshield, consistent with the customer's claim of a crack spreading from a stone impact. Image img_2 shows the same car but does not show the damage.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the claimed object (side mirror) and the claimed damage (broken).
claim_status_justification: The image 'img_1' clearly shows the side mirror of a red car with significant cracks and breakage, supporting the customer's claim that the side mirror got damaged and is not sitting correctly.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Images clearly show the claimed object and part, allowing for assessment of damage.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows multiple scratches and scuff marks on the rear bumper/quarter panel area of the silver car, consistent with the customer's claim of 'bumper damage' and 'tapped from behind'. Image img_2 provides a wider view of the rear of the car, confirming the location.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images do not show the claimed headlight clearly enough to inspect for cracks. The sun glare and distance obscure the relevant part.
claim_status_justification: The user claims a cracked headlight. Image img_1 shows a car from a distance with significant sun glare directly in front, making it impossible to discern any detail on the headlight. The cropped images also do not provide a clear view of the headlight to assess for cracks.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 1 is too blurry to assess any damage. Image 2 clearly shows a dent on the door panel, but image 1 does not provide sufficient context to confirm it's the same vehicle or the claimed side.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims a dent on the door panel. Image 1 is too blurry to identify any damage or even the specific car. Image 2 clearly shows a dent on a car's door panel, which aligns with the claim. However, due to the blurriness of img_1, it's not possible to confirm if both images are of the same vehicle or if img_2 represents the 'side' of the car as described in the context of img_1. Therefore, there is not enough information to fully support the claim across both images.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images do not clearly show the hood of the car where the scratch is claimed. The car is severely damaged in the front, making it difficult to assess a scratch on the hood.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims a 'scratch across the top panel' (hood). However, the provided image (img_1) shows a severely damaged front of a car, with the hood open and significant structural damage. It is impossible to discern any scratch on the hood due to the overall damage and the angle of the photo.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the claimed object (laptop) and the claimed part (screen) with visible damage.
claim_status_justification: The image 'img_1' clearly shows a laptop with a severely shattered screen, consistent with the customer's claim of a cracked display glass after the laptop fell.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows the claimed hinge damage.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows the laptop hinge area is broken and detached from the screen bezel, which supports the claim that the hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. Image img_2 provides a wider view of the laptop.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show the claimed object (laptop) and the claimed part (keyboard) with visible water damage.
claim_status_justification: The images (img_1 and its crops) clearly show water spilled across the laptop keyboard, consistent with the customer's claim of water damage and sticky keys. The water droplets are visible on and between the keys.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_2 clearly shows a dent on the corner of the laptop, matching the claim.
claim_status_justification: Image img_2 clearly shows a dent on the corner of the laptop, which directly supports the customer's claim of a dent on one corner of the laptop. Image img_1 shows the overall laptop but not the specific damage.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show the claimed object (laptop) and the claimed part (screen) with visible damage.
claim_status_justification: The user claims screen damage, specifically that the screen looks shattered. Image img_1 and its crops clearly show a MacBook Pro laptop with a severely shattered screen, consistent with the user's description.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the claimed object part and the alleged damage.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows a scratch on the trackpad area of the laptop, which aligns with the customer's claim of physical damage around the trackpad area.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show the claimed package corner and the damage.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims one corner of the delivery box was crushed. Image img_1 clearly shows a crushed corner on the cardboard package, consistent with the claim.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show the claimed object and the damaged part.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows a brown cardboard package with significant tearing and damage to the tape seal on one side, consistent with the customer's claim of 'torn packaging' and 'seal wali side phati hui thi'. Image img_2 shows an undamaged, generic cardboard box, which does not contradict the claim as it appears to be a reference image.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show the claimed package and the alleged water damage.
claim_status_justification: The images (img_1) clearly show a cardboard box with significant dark staining and warping consistent with water damage on multiple sides and corners, supporting the customer's claim of a 'wet-looking stain' and 'package surface looked damaged'.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows the inside of an opened package with only crumpled paper, indicating missing contents.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 shows an opened cardboard box filled with crumpled paper, with no product visible. This supports the claim that the item ordered was not inside the box and the contents are missing. Image img_2 shows the exterior of a sealed package, which does not contradict the claim as the claim is about the contents after opening.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images show a damaged can, not the claimed crushed shipping box.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims the shipping box was crushed, but the provided image (img_1) clearly shows a crushed can, which is an item inside a package, not the shipping box itself. This contradicts the claim.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Images clearly show the claimed damage to the package seal.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows the security seal on the package is torn and the cardboard underneath is also torn, consistent with the customer's claim of a 'torn-open package'. Image img_2 shows a similar package with an intact security seal, providing a comparison.
